title: Postgres VACUUM tuning

performance_problem.metric_impact: Bloat 40% of disk; queries scan dead rows

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. autovacuum default conservative; tune per high-update table.
2. pg_repack for online rebuild (see migration-211).
3. fillfactor lower = HOT updates.
trade_offs: More vacuum = more IO during peaks
what_breaks_naive_approach: Default settings for write-heavy: bloat accumulates.

title: Avoiding prop drilling causing re-renders

performance_problem.symptom: Deep props change causes intermediate re-renders

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Context for cross-cutting; not just to skip prop drilling.
2. Lift state up properly; consider composition.
3. children prop pattern: parents render unchanged subtree.

title: Compressing API responses with Brotli
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Brotli compresses better than gzip.
2. Skip for already-compressed (images).
3. Set Vary: Accept-Encoding.

title: Reducing TTFB by caching at edge
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. CDN caches at edge.
2. SWR: serve stale fast, revalidate.
3. Origin compute moves to edge functions where possible.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Origin hit per request: distance latency adds up.

title: WeakRef for cache
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. WeakRef cache allows GC.
2. FinalizationRegistry cleans up cache entries when GC'd.
3. Or use LRU with size bound.
trade_offs: No control over when GC; cache miss possible anytime
what_breaks_naive_approach: Strong ref + no bound = unbounded heap.

title: http.Agent connection pooling

performance_problem.symptom: Many outbound HTTP requests; TCP setup dominant

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. http.Agent keepAlive: true reuses connections.
2. maxSockets controls concurrency.
3. Per-target Agent for isolation.

title: Index-only scans
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. INCLUDE in index brings columns into index.
2. Visibility map enables skipping heap for hot rows.
3. SELECT only what's in index for IOS.

title: Avoiding inline object/array props
diagnosis_steps: Prop is new ref each render
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. {} or [] in JSX = new ref each render.
2. useMemo stabilizes.
3. Or move out of component scope (constants).
trade_offs: useMemo cost; only worth for memo'd children

title: Lazy-loading polyfills

performance_problem.symptom: All polyfills loaded even on modern browsers

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Use polyfill.io or @web/polyfills-loader.
2. Detect feature, load polyfill if missing.
3. Module/nomodule pattern: modern gets ESM, legacy gets transpiled.
trade_offs: Detection adds 1 extra round-trip on old

title: Server-Timing header for backend insights
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Server-Timing exposes server-side timings to browser DevTools.
2. Visible in Network panel.
3. RUM can capture.
what_breaks_naive_approach: No insight into where time goes.

title: React.startTransition for non-urgent UI
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. startTransition for heavy non-urgent.
2. urgent: typing, click responses.
3. non-urgent: filtered results, etc.
4. isPending shows progress.

title: Avoiding SELECT * in performance-critical paths
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Project only needed columns.
2. Enables IOS.
3. Less network.

title: Prefetching next-page data
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Prefetch next page during idle.
2. TanStack's prefetchQuery.
3. requestIdleCallback or hover signal.
trade_offs: Speculative fetch wastes if not used

title: Self-hosting Google Fonts

performance_problem.symptom: Google Fonts adds extra DNS + connections

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Self-host = single origin = less negotiation.
2. No third-party tracking.
3. Match exact metrics via size-adjust.

title: Event emitter listener leaks
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. on() adds listener; off() must follow.
2. once() auto-removes.
3. setMaxListeners helps debug.

title: Cluster mode for multi-core
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Cluster forks per core.
2. Sticky for stateful (websockets).
3. PM2 or k8s replicas vs cluster — pick one.
trade_offs: In-memory state per worker; sticky needed for some

title: Avoiding unnecessary state
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Derive in render.
2. useState only for truly local state.
3. Lift only when shared.

title: Code splitting by route + component
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Lazy at component boundary for rare paths.
2. Prefetch on hover/intent.

title: Avoiding cross-database joins

performance_problem.symptom: Cross-DB join via API loop slow

diagnosis_steps: Service A loops calling service B
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Batch endpoint: POST /users/batch with ids.
2. Async events for projections.
3. CDC for cross-service reads.

title: GraphQL query batching

performance_problem.symptom: Many GraphQL queries from same page

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Apollo Client batches queries fired in same tick.
2. Server merges into single response.
3. Persisted queries cut bandwidth.

title: Avoiding context for high-frequency updates

performance_problem.symptom: Mouse position in context = all re-render

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. High-freq state → external store (Zustand, Jotai).
2. Consumers subscribe to slices.
3. Or imperative (refs).

title: Hydration mismatch detection
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. SSR must match client first render.
2. Defer client-only to useEffect.
3. Avoid non-deterministic in render.
solution: // see test-137

title: Detached DOM nodes
anti_patterns_to_avoid: DOM ref in JS scope after remove
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. After removeChild, JS refs prevent GC.
2. Null out refs in cleanup.

title: Avoiding catalog scans
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Prepared statements skip catalog scan.
2. Connection pool reuse maintains.
solution: // see migration-189

title: AVIF / WebP for images
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. AVIF smallest; WebP fallback; JPEG last.
2. <picture> with type sources.
3. CDN auto-converts (Cloudinary, Cloudflare Images).

title: Suspense boundaries placement
diagnosis_steps: Child takes a moment → whole tree fallback
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Suspense isolates pending state.
2. Per-section boundary keeps rest visible.
3. Skeleton matches final shape.
solution: // see test-179

title: Optimizing CORS preflight
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Max-Age caches preflight per (origin, path).
2. 600s reasonable.
3. Avoid headers triggering preflight when possible.
solution: // see migration-249

title: Avoiding excessive logging
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. pino is fast (async serialization).
2. Sample debug logs.
3. Don't log body bytes; log keys/sizes.

title: Avoiding ORDER BY without index
anti_patterns_to_avoid: ASC index for DESC query (PG fine; some DBs not)
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Index in query order.
2. PG B-tree both directions usable.
3. Compound: WHERE cols first, ORDER cols last.
solution:
-- query: WHERE customer_id ORDER BY created_at DESC
CREATE INDEX idx ON orders (customer_id, created_at DESC);

title: Performance attribution
diagnosis_steps: RUM data shows slow LCP but no breakdown
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. web-vitals v4+ exposes attribution.
2. Per-metric: which element/script.
